rows.definition: abi.VerdictRequireWitness is the kernel verdict kind that leaves a gated call PENDING an independent read-back, so the kernel must corroborate the caller's claimed effect (carried in the WitnessPayload) before the call may dispatch.
rows.distinction: It is the request-for-proof signal, not the proof's answer: unlike a WitnessOutcome it carries no confirmed/refuted/abstain result, it is the intermediate verdict that triggers asking a resolver.

rows.definition: abi.ReasonUnwitnessed is the deny reason-code the kernel attaches when a require-witness gate found no corroboration, so the fail-closed default turns the pending verdict into a DENY (also surfaced as looprecover's unwitnessed disposition).
rows.distinction: It is the terminal DENY label for 'no proof arrived', not the resolver's neutral WitnessAbstain which merely reports 'no evidence either way' before the gate decides.

rows.definition: abi.WitnessPayload is the typed bounded-disclosure verdict payload struct{Claim string} that an adjudicator attaches to a RequireWitness or Deny verdict to carry the single claim string the kernel must corroborate.
rows.distinction: It is the wire carrier wrapping ONE claim-grammar string on a verdict, not the Claim concept itself nor the multi-field taskmgr WitnessRecord.

rows.definition: refute is the action of calling vdso.Revoke(witness) on the integrity/coherence bus to mark a previously-trusted world-state witness as contradicted, stranding every cache entry admitted under it.
rows.distinction: It is the verb/trigger that PRODUCES a refutation, not the recorded Revocation event nor the TrustEpoch counter that tallies how many have fired.

rows.definition: callavoid.ProofRefuted is the two-valued proof-status value 'REFUTED' meaning a cost-benefit proof concluded that avoidance/memoization does not pay (single-use, too-volatile, or validation as costly as the call).
rows.distinction: It is an economic break-even verdict about whether caching is worth it, NOT a world-state WitnessRefuted where external evidence contradicted a claim.

rows.definition: taskmgr.WitnessRecord is the evidence-backed verdict struct a Witness attaches BESIDE a task/step's claimed State (VerifiedState plus source and evidence refs), so a snapshot holds both 'process says done' and 'a witness has/has not confirmed it'.
rows.distinction: It is the full multi-field evidence record kept as a separate rung from the claimed State, not the tiny three-valued abi WitnessOutcome enum the in-process gate folds.

rows.definition: abi.WitnessConfirmed is the WitnessOutcome value meaning a resolver INDEPENDENTLY CORROBORATED the claimed effect from evidence the agent did not author, which opens the require-witness gate.
rows.distinction: It is the gate-opening positive of the outcome triple, distinct from WitnessRefuted (evidence contradicts) and WitnessAbstain (no evidence either way).

rows.definition: abi.WitnessRefuted is the WitnessOutcome value meaning a resolver found evidence that CONTRADICTS the claimed effect (the agent's claim was false), keeping the require-witness gate closed.
rows.distinction: It is the evidence-contradicts verdict in the world-state witness triple, NOT callavoid's economic ProofRefuted nor the recorded refutation event on the vDSO bus.

rows.definition: abi.WitnessAbstain is the zero-value WitnessOutcome meaning the resolver had NO evidence either way (fail-to-abstain), e.g. an unrecognized claim or git unavailable, which the kernel's fail-closed default then turns into a deny.
rows.distinction: Abstain means the resolver ran but found nothing decisive, distinct from VerifiedUnavailable where the witness could not be consulted at all (no network/missing ref).

rows.definition: vdso.defaultWitnessOf is the default extractor that derives the external world-state witness string a call/result was admitted under (from the call/result meta), used by the coherence bus when no per-tool adapter is registered.
rows.distinction: It is the extraction FUNCTION that reads out a witness identifier, not the world-state-witness value itself nor the per-tool WitnessFunc override that can replace it.

rows.definition: kernel.resolveWitness drives the require-witness gate: on a RequireWitness verdict it asks every registered abi.WitnessResolver to corroborate the claim and folds their WitnessOutcomes into a final allow/deny.
rows.distinction: It is the kernel-side DRIVER that consults resolvers, not the abi.WitnessResolver interface it calls, and not the same-named vdso.resolveWitness that merely extracts a witness string for the cache bus.

rows.definition: cachemeta.ShapeGLMTurnSegmentWitnessed is the simplest live-path coherence shaper: given a turn's prompt segments and a revoked(witness) predicate, it emits the prefix-break directive that severs the provider prefix at the first refuted span.
rows.distinction: It is the prefix-breaking SHAPER that consumes refutation state to keep a GLM prompt coherent, not the witness value nor the Revocation event that refuted a span.

rows.definition: loopmgr.StatusWitnessedDone is the loop run-status 'witnessed_done' meaning an independent witness event confirmed the run's claimed completion (carrying evidence refs), promoting it above claimed_done.
rows.distinction: It is the loop-manager's per-run promoted status proving completion, not the abi WitnessConfirmed outcome of a single gate nor the claimed_done self-report it supersedes.

rows.definition: vdso.SubscribeRevocations registers an observer callback that fires on every refutation (Revocation) published to the integrity bus and returns a cancel func, never firing on the read hot path.
rows.distinction: It is the SUBSCRIPTION seam for watching refutations, not a Revocation event itself nor the refute action that triggers one.

rows.definition: sessionimage.WitnessEntry binds one effect's keep-bit (a taskmgr.WitnessRecord) to a stable EffectID inside a persisted/restored session image, so a rehydrated session knows which effects were witnessed.
rows.distinction: It is a per-effect persistence row in a session image keyed by EffectID, not taskmgr's live WitnessRecord verdict it wraps nor the claim it corroborates.

rows.definition: vdso.WitnessFunc is a per-tool adapter func(c *ToolCall, r *Result) string registered via RegisterWitness that extracts the external world-state witness a specific tool's result was admitted under, overriding the default extraction.
rows.distinction: It is the per-tool OVERRIDE that customizes witness extraction, distinct from the fallback defaultWitnessOf and from taskmgr's identically-named WitnessFunc that adapts a func to the Witness interface.

rows.definition: WitnessGaps is the TUI loop-overview counter tallying runs that ended or claimed done but were never witnessed — the per-report aggregate of the unwitnessed gap, weighted into the attention score.
rows.distinction: It is the dashboard AGGREGATE count of missing witnesses, not a single run's unwitnessed disposition nor the ReasonUnwitnessed deny code.

rows.definition: loopmgr.StatusWitnessRefused is the loop run-status 'witness_refused' (and its WitnessRefused counter) meaning a witness RAN and the evidence contradicted the run's claimed completion.
rows.distinction: It is the loop-manager's run-level refusal status, the operational dual of the abi.WitnessRefuted outcome enum the in-process gate uses.

rows.definition: vdso.MissWitnessRevoked is the cache-miss reason 'WITNESS_REVOKED' counted when a cached entry was admitted under a now-refuted external witness and therefore fails closed instead of serving stale bytes.
rows.distinction: It is the CACHE-side miss classification for serving under a refuted witness, not the Revocation event that refuted it nor the gate's WitnessRefuted outcome.

rows.definition: taskmgr.VerifiedUnavailable (and loopmgr.StatusWitnessUnavailable) is the state where a witness was ASKED but could not read the effect back — no network, a missing ref — so the claim is neither confirmed nor refused and must not downgrade to claimed-done.
rows.distinction: Unavailable means the witness could not be consulted, distinct from WitnessAbstain where the resolver ran but found no decisive evidence.

rows.definition: dgxGlmGpuWitness is the on-DGX CUDA GPU correctness witness for the GLM-MoE-DSA backend forward (TestCUDAGLMMoeDsaBackendForward run via tools/dgx_glm_gpu_witness.sh), invoked because the win32 build host cannot execute the CUDA path.
rows.distinction: It is a hardware-run correctness PROOF artifact for a GPU kernel, not the kernel's require-witness trust rung nor a world-state-witness on the cache coherence bus.
